county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Grayson County formed 1846 from Fannin. County seat is Sherman. North Texas / Blackland Prairie region with farming and urban growth heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Sherman. North Texas county. Sherman Museum. Grayson County Frontier Village. Significant Red River heritage. Peter's Colony records at TSLAC. Austin College archives. Check Fannin for pre-1846 records. Major Civil War unrest (Cooke County hangings affected region).
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Grayson County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1846; County Clerk in Sherman holds deed books

source_profiles.source_name: Grayson County Deed Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1846 to present. Continuous coverage from county formation.

source_profiles.source_name: Grayson County Marriage Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses from 1846. Texas county clerks have maintained marriage records since county formation. State registration from 1966.

source_profiles.source_name: Grayson County Probate Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Probate records, wills, estate inventories, and guardianships from 1846. Texas probate records are maintained at the county level by the County Clerk.

source_profiles.source_name: Grayson County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1850 to 1950 for Grayson County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.source_name: Grayson County Enslaved Persons Records -- TSLAC / County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Estate inventories listing enslaved persons, bills of sale, tax rolls listing slaveholders and enslaved persons counts, and Freedmen's Bureau records (NARA). Grayson County was part of the Texas plantation economy.

source_profiles.source_name: Texas General Land Office -- Grayson County Land Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: State land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Grayson County from 1846. GLO archives include searchable online databases.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Gregg County formed 1873 from Rusk, Upshur. County seat is Longview. East Texas / Piney Woods region with timber industry and plantation heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Longview. East Texas oil county. Gregg County Historical Museum. East Texas Oil Museum at Kilgore College. Longview was a major East Texas oil boom center (1930s). Significant African American community. Check Rusk and Upshur for pre-1873 records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Gregg County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1873; County Clerk in Longview holds deed books

source_profiles.source_name: Gregg County Deed Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1873 to present. Continuous coverage from county formation.

source_profiles.source_name: Gregg County Marriage Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses from 1873. Texas county clerks have maintained marriage records since county formation. State registration from 1966.

source_profiles.source_name: Gregg County Probate Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Probate records, wills, estate inventories, and guardianships from 1873. Texas probate records are maintained at the county level by the County Clerk.

source_profiles.source_name: Gregg County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1873 to 1950 for Gregg County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.source_name: Gregg County Oil and Gas Lease Records -- GLO / County Clerk
source_profiles.repository: Texas General Land Office, Austin / County Clerk, Longview
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Oil and gas lease records, mineral rights transfers, and related land documents from 1930. Texas GLO maintains state mineral records. County Clerk has private mineral lease records.

source_profiles.source_name: Texas General Land Office -- Gregg County Land Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: State land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Gregg County from 1873. GLO archives include searchable online databases.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Grimes County formed 1846 from Montgomery. County seat is Anderson. Central Texas region with farming, ranching, and diverse immigrant heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Anderson. Central Texas county. Grimes County Historical Commission. Gibbons Creek settlement — early African American community. Plantation economy. Check Montgomery County for pre-1846 records. Prairie View A&M University (historically Black university) nearby.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Grimes County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1846; County Clerk in Anderson holds deed books

source_profiles.coverage_notes: State land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Grimes County from 1846. GLO archives include searchable online databases.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Guadalupe County formed 1846 from Bexar, Gonzales. County seat is Seguin. Central Texas region with farming, ranching, and diverse immigrant heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Seguin. South-central Texas county with German settlement heritage. Seguin Heritage Museum. Sebastopol House State Historic Site. Check Bexar and Gonzales for pre-1846 records. German and Mexican-American communities. Juan Seguin history.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Guadalupe County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1846; County Clerk in Seguin holds deed books

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Estate inventories listing enslaved persons, bills of sale, tax rolls listing slaveholders and enslaved persons counts, and Freedmen's Bureau records (NARA). Guadalupe County was part of the Texas plantation economy.

source_profiles.source_name: Guadalupe County German Church Records (Lutheran, Catholic, Methodist)
source_profiles.repository: Local congregations / TSLAC / Texas Lutheran University archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: German immigrant church records including baptisms, confirmations, marriages, and burials. Guadalupe County has significant German settlement heritage. German-language records may require translation. Check local Lutheran, Catholic, and Methodist congregations.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: State land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Guadalupe County from 1846. GLO archives include searchable online databases.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Plainview. South Plains county organized 1888. Hale County Farm and Ranch Museum. Llano Estacado Museum at Wayland Baptist University. Farming (cotton, grain). Check Bexar for pre-organization records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Hall County formed 1876 from Bexar, Fannin. County seat is Memphis. Texas Panhandle / South Plains region with ranching and farming heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Memphis. Rolling Plains county organized 1890. Heritage Hall Museum. Cotton farming and ranching. Check Bexar and Fannin for pre-organization records. Hall County Historical Commission.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Hamilton County formed 1858 from Bosque, Comanche, Lampasas. County seat is Hamilton. Central Texas region with farming, ranching, and diverse immigrant heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Hamilton. Central Texas county. Hamilton County Museum. Check Bosque, Comanche, Lampasas for pre-1858 records. Farming and ranching. Hico claims Billy the Kid heritage.

source_profiles.source_name: Native American Records -- Hamilton County Area (various tribes)
source_profiles.repository: NARA, Fort Worth / Oklahoma Historical Society
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Pre-settlement and frontier-era records for various tribes in the Hamilton County area. Includes Indian Affairs records, military campaign records, and reservation records at NARA Fort Worth. Texas frontier counties experienced significant Native American conflict through the 1870s.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Hansford County formed 1876 from Bexar. County seat is Spearman. Texas Panhandle / South Plains region with ranching and farming heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Spearman. North Panhandle county organized 1889. Stationmaster's House Museum. Farming and ranching. Check Bexar for pre-organization records. One of the last Panhandle counties organized.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Hardeman County formed 1858 from Fannin. County seat is Quanah. North Texas frontier region with ranching and Indian defense heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Quanah. North Texas frontier county organized 1884. Hardeman County Historical Museum. Named for Texas pioneers. Quanah Parker (last Comanche chief) heritage. Check Fannin for pre-organization records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Hardin County formed 1858 from Jefferson, Liberty. County seat is Kountze. East Texas / Piney Woods region with timber industry and plantation heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Kountze. East Texas timber county. Big Thicket National Preserve area — check NPS for ecological/settlement records. Hardin County Historical Commission. Lumber industry employment records. Check Jefferson and Liberty for pre-1858 records.
